summary: Ask a new pilot what scares them most and the majority will say the radio—they worry about saying the wrong thing, interrupting someone, or sounding like a beginner.

title: AOPA backs bill to equip all contract towers with ADS-B
summary: A bipartisan group of senators introduced the Air Traffic Situational Awareness Enhancement Act, which would equip more than 90 air traffic control towers operating without radar or other surveillance technology with a cost-effective ADS-B-based solution.

summary: WASHINGTON—The Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA) welcomes the introduction of the Air Traffic Situational Awareness Enhancement Act, which would equip the remaining 90+ air traffic control towers without radar or other surveillance technology with a cost-effective ...

title: AV-30-C gains ForeFlight integration, advanced autopilot
summary: Certified aircraft equipped with the uAvionix AV-30-C can now wirelessly receive flight plans and enhance navigation capability.

title: AOPA Statement on House Vote on ROTOR Act
summary: WASHINGTON—The Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA) issued the following statement from Senior Vice President of Government Affairs and Advocacy Jim Coon after the House of Representatives did not pass the Rotorcraft Operations Transparency and Oversight Reform (ROTOR...

title: Training and Safety Tip: 'Decisions and revisions'
summary: There’s an old saying that takeoffs are optional, but landings…well…not so much. Within the constraints of remaining fuel, choosing the time and place of landing is entirely up to you as pilot in command.

title: Legends that lift
summary: The logbook of Ricardo Astillero II holds the signature of Tuskegee-era instructor Roscoe Draper—and a letter of recommendation from another aviation giant, Les Morris.

title: AOPA’s Air Safety Institute Releases Early Analysis of Steamboat Springs, Colorado Crash
summary: FREDERICK, Md.—The Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association Air Safety Institute (ASI) has released a new video providing an early look at last week’s crash that left four people dead south of Steamboat Springs, Colorado.

title: Finding a smooth ride
summary: The FAA’s Aviation Weather Demonstration and Evaluation Services wants to know how you determine whether there’s turbulence along your route of flight and which features are most valuable to you when making that determination.

title: ‘Incompatible’ land use continues in Colorado town
summary: Ground was broken on a 200-apartment complex less than half a mile from the end of Runway 29 at Vance Brand Airport in Longmont, Colorado, despite the developer never filing appropriate paperwork with the FAA.

title: NTSB chair voices support for PAPA
summary: NTSB Chairwoman Jennifer Homendy voiced support for the Pilot and Aircraft Privacy Act (PAPA) in an exchange with Sen. Ted Budd (R-N.C.) during a February 12 Senate Commerce, Science, and Transportation Committee hearing on aviation safety and the midair collision between a mi...

title: Alaska weather charts upgraded
summary: The National Weather Service’s Alaska Aviation Weather Unit is making some significant changes to its Surface and Significant Weather charts.

title: Training and Safety Tip: Snow room for error
summary: The winter months pose a unique set of challenges for pilots flying in regions where snow and ice are common.

title: Added awareness where pilots need it most
summary: Misjudging height above the surface is one of the most unforgiving errors in general aviation, and Garmin's GHA 15 height advisor brings certified radar-based height-above-ground awareness to a broad range of GA aircraft.

title: Arizona legislation would limit ADS-B misuse
summary: A bill authored by local pilot CJ deVries and introduced by state Rep. Matt Gress (R-District 4) would prohibit the state, its political subdivisions, or private entities from using information transmitted through an aircraft collision avoidance safety system known as ADS-B to...

title: How debriefs build better pilots
summary: The engine shuts down, the postflight is done. The instructor and student walk back into the building to grab a coffee, talk briefly, and go on to the next flight or home. Or maybe, just chat on the walk back inside, and call it a day.

title: Training and Safety Tip: Fly in safe spaces
summary: Following a defined visual or instrument approach to any airport creates a reasonable expectation you will remain clear of any obstructions. The FAA has established rules and regulations regarding airport environments, including established approaches—both instrument and...

title: Super Bowl procedures in effect at many California airports
summary: Flying general aviation in the San Francisco Bay Area through February 10, 2026? If your intended destination is on the FAA’s list of airports implementing Super Bowl procedures, you’re strongly encouraged to contact the airport or FBO to ensure space, confirm requ...

title: Congress passes FAA funding bill
summary: A new government funding bill signed into law on February 4 will deliver major investments in air traffic control modernization, controller staffing, and addressing medical certification delays—while also blocking any effort to privatize the ATC system.

title: Denver pilots shocked by hangar rate hike
summary: Hangar tenants facing an 82-percent rent increase contacted AOPA for help, and when the Denver FBO failed to justify the new rate under the FAA "fair and reasonable" standard, the affected pilots proceeded to file an airport compliance complaint under FAR Part 13.

title: Alton Bay opens ice runway for the 2026 season
summary: Aircraft began operating on the lower 48's only officially recognized ice runway at Alton Bay Seaplane Base following its late January opening.

title: AOPA Announces Leadership Transition as CEO Moves to Advisory Role
summary: FREDERICK, Md.—The Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA) today announced a leadership transition as President and CEO Darren Pleasance steps out of the day-to-day CEO role and into an advisory capacity to support AOPA and the Board of Trustees during the search for AOP...

title: Utah airport reforms hangar lease policy
summary: After nearly a year of public comment, meetings, and federal review, airport officials in southern Utah have adopted a revised hangar leasing policy (also known as Non-Commercial Hangar Leasing Policy) at St. George Regional Airport, following joint advocacy by AOPA and engage...

title: Missionary aviation groups offer youth summer camps
summary: Seventy years after pioneering missionary pilot Nate Saint and four fellow missionaries lost their lives following their calling, the organization Saint served is among a few groups offering summer camps to train and inspire a new generation of pilots to follow in Saint's foot...

title: Assessing weather conditions at rural airports
summary: For many GA pilots, the first decision of the day happens long before the preflight inspection and the turning of the keys.

title: Training and Safety Tip: Check your boots
summary: As the seasons change and temperatures drop, it’s normal for tire pressure to decrease slightly because colder air is more dense than warmer air.

title: Power through learning plateaus
summary: Learning plateaus are a normal part of flight training, but that doesn’t make them any less frustrating or discouraging.
